etymology.description: Originally 𦔻 -> ideogrammic compound (耳 ('ear') + 口 ('mouths')) -> Later as phono-semantic: phonetic 𡈼 + semantic 耳 ('ear') + semantic 㥁
etymology.interpretation: 聽(tīng, 'listen; hear') combines multiple elements related to the act of listening: 耳(耳) (ěr, 'ear') represents the organ involved, while 𡈼 provides the phonetic cue. The additional semantic element 㥁 emphasizes the idea of focused attention or presence, completing the concept of listening carefully.
etymology.simplification: The traditional character 聽 was simplified to 听 by adopting the unrelated earlier character 𠯸, which was itself stylized and phoneticized in modern simplification processes.

examples.chinese: 我喜欢听音乐(我喜歡聽音樂)。
examples.english: I like to listen to music.

examples.chinese: 请仔细听讲(請仔細聽講)，保持安静(保持安靜)。
examples.english: Please listen carefully and keep quiet.

examples.chinese: 孩子们坐下(孩子們坐下)，安静地听故事(安靜地聽故事)。
examples.pinyin: Háizimen zuò xià，ānjìng de tīng gùshì.
examples.english: The children sat down and listened quietly to the story.
